Reorder AI provider priority for optimal performance

New priority (best → worst):
1. Groq (PRIMARY) - Fast, generous free tier ⚡
2. OpenRouter (FALLBACK) - Multiple free models 🔄
3. Gemini (BACKUP) - Paid backup once quota resets 🆘

Why this order:
- Groq: Most stable free tier, fastest inference
- OpenRouter: Multiple model options as fallback
- Gemini: Only use after quota recovery (24h)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix nginx 403 on static assets: set home dir to o+x

nginx runs as www-data and needs execute permission on every
directory in the path to /static. Without chmod o+x on the home
directory the entire /static location returns 403 Forbidden,
leaving the UI with bare HTML and no CSS.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(hardening): round 6 — 10 weaknesses fixed with TDD

W1: /load-script path containment check + symlink resolution (RCE prevention)
W2: Dashboard eval-file resolves symlinks before containment check
W3: Replace Task.Wait() with do! in SSE snapshot replay (thread pool starvation)
W4: 4MB body size cap in readJsonProp/readJsonBody (OOM prevention)
W5: ManifestPersistence payloadEnd uses List.min (correct multi-section boundary)
W6: StandbySession.BaseUrl threaded through StandbyReady — WorkerBaseUrl populated after hot-swap
W7: getWarmupContextForElm uses 5s linked CancellationToken (was 100s default)
W8: DaemonSessionStopped event only written inside Ok branch of StopSession
W9: proxyPost gates body read on ContentLength <= 1MB (OOM proxy prevention)
W10: FeaturePushState.KnownBindings maintained incrementally in recordEval (O(1) vs O(n))

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Phase 8.6: Package Rust library build as go generate step

Implements ROADMAP.md Phase 8.6 - enables 'go generate && go build' workflow
for building the full static binary without requiring Make.

Changes:
- Created internal/render/generate.go with go:generate directive
- Added scripts/build-rust.sh comprehensive build automation script
  * Auto-detects host architecture and musl Rust target
  * Checks for required tools (musl-gcc, cargo, rustup)
  * Auto-installs musl Rust target if missing
  * Builds Rust static library (librender.a)
  * Compiles musl compatibility stub (dl_find_object_stub.o)
- Updated README.md with go generate workflow documentation
- Updated ROADMAP.md to mark Phase 8.6 as complete
- Added scripts/README.md with comprehensive documentation

Workflow:
1. go generate ./...  # builds Rust artifacts
2. go build ./...     # builds Go binary with CGO linking

Integration:
- Fully compatible with existing Makefile workflow
- Zero regressions in code complexity metrics
- All tests passing (except pre-existing GPU hardware tests)
- Static binary verified (ldd reports 'not a dynamic executable')

Benefits:
- Enables Go-native build pipelines
- Simplifies CI/CD integration for teams using go tooling
- Maintains full compatibility with make build
- Clear error messages for missing dependencies

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix re-run reliability and supervisor process lifecycle

deploy.sh:
- Use ps+awk+xargs to stop minio before re-runs
- Shut down existing supervisord before starting new instance to
  prevent stale uwsgi workers from holding ports 5000 and 5001
- Remove orphaned uwsgi/minio processes not tracked by supervisor
- Ensure log files in /tmp are owned by the deploy user to prevent
  permission errors when supervisord runs as non-root
- Increase post-supervisord sleep to 8s for stable startup

invenio-supervisor.conf:
- Add stopasgroup + killasgroup to all programs so supervisor sends
  signals to the whole process group, not just the master process;
  prevents uwsgi workers from becoming orphans on shutdown

invenio-rdm/scripts/setup.sh:
- Make invenio db create idempotent (skip if tables already exist)
- Make invenio rdm fixtures idempotent (skip if already loaded)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: install ca-certificates in runtime Docker image for HTTPS support (#1689)

`debian:bookworm-slim` ships without root CA certificates, causing Go to
fail with `x509: certificate signed by unknown authority` when Manael
proxies requests to HTTPS origins.

## Changes

- **`Dockerfile`**: add `ca-certificates` to the runtime stage's
`apt-get install`, co-located with the existing `libvips42` install to
avoid an extra layer.

```dockerfile
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        ca-certificates \
        libvips42 \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
```

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Docker: Fix x509 certificate error when fetching from
HTTPS origins</issue_title>
> <issue_description>### Background
> When Manael v3.0 proxies requests to an origin server that uses HTTPS,
it fails with the following error:
> `x509: certificate signed by unknown authority`
> 
> This happens because the runtime Docker image (based on `debian-slim`
or similar minimal images) does not include root CA certificates by
default. The previous `distroless` image included these certificates
out-of-the-box.
> 
> ### Proposal
> Install the `ca-certificates` package in the final runtime stage of
the Dockerfile so that Go can securely verify HTTPS connections.
> 
> ### Tasks
> - Open `Dockerfile`.
> - In the final runtime stage, add a command to install
`ca-certificates`.
> - Clean up `apt` cache to keep the image size minimal.
> 
> ### Example Update (`Dockerfile`)
> ```dockerfile
> # ... (builder stage remains the same)
> 
> # Final runtime stage
> FROM debian:bookworm-slim
> 
> # Add this step to install CA certificates
> RUN apt-get update && \
>     apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends ca-certificates && \
>     rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
> 
> COPY --from=builder /go/bin/manael /usr/local/bin/
> 
> # ...
> ```</issue_description>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> </comments>
> 


</details>

fix(lib-client): force fee=0 for TokenCreation system transactions (#1808)

* fix(lib-client): force fee=0 for TokenCreation system transactions

build_create_token_tx was performing a two-pass sign→estimate-fee→re-sign
pattern that overwrote fee=0 with a size-based non-zero fee. Consensus
validation rejects TokenCreation with fee != 0 (system tx invariant),
causing every mobile token creation submission to fail.

Remove the fee estimation pass entirely; TokenCreation is always fee-free.

* Update lib-client/src/token_tx.rs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Updated ROADMAP.md: marked Phase 8.5 as complete

commit.message:
fix(ml-pipeline): thread-safe ONNX sessions, param mapping parity, normalization docs

OnnxInferenceService: Add try-catch safety around old session disposal in
ReplaceSession and Dispose to prevent one failed native cleanup from breaking
the service. Add comprehensive thread-safety contract documentation.

AzureMLTrainingService: Forward --hidden-size and --sequence-length from
LstmHyperparameters to both C# container and legacy Python container paths.
Previously these params existed in config but were never sent to training scripts.

GoldAIG.Training.Azure: Register --hidden-size and --sequence-length CLI options
so the container accepts them without argument errors (uses compiled defaults).

train_lstm.py (Azure ML): Add --hidden-size argument and thread it through
train_model() to LSTMPricePredictor instead of hardcoding 128.

MachineLearningService: Replace terse normalization note with comprehensive
alignment table covering all 9 ML pipelines (FastTree, LightGBM, SDCA, LSTM,
Python, Quantum). Add LightGBM scale-invariance documentation.

commit.message:
feat(eslint-markdown): add `blankLineAbove` and `blankLineBelow` option in `consistent-code-style` rule (#501)

This pull request introduces support for enforcing blank lines above and
below code blocks in the `consistent-code-style` rule for the
`eslint-markdown` package, aligning its behavior with `markdownlint`'s
`MD031` rule. It also adds a utility for detecting blank lines, expands
rule options and tests, and updates documentation and repo conventions
to improve clarity and consistency.

**Enhancements to `eslint-markdown` code style rule:**

* Added `blankLineAbove` and `blankLineBelow` options to the
`consistent-code-style` rule, allowing users to specify the required
number of blank lines above and below fenced code blocks (excluding
indented code), matching `markdownlint`'s `MD031` behavior.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-9e6e6cfa402aae1e2be9ed59d63fa3d67420841a121cef36591b5cb1e711a830L29-R31)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-9e6e6cfa402aae1e2be9ed59d63fa3d67420841a121cef36591b5cb1e711a830R80-R101)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-9e6e6cfa402aae1e2be9ed59d63fa3d67420841a121cef36591b5cb1e711a830R110-R120)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-9e6e6cfa402aae1e2be9ed59d63fa3d67420841a121cef36591b5cb1e711a830L101-R141)
[[5]](diffhunk://#diff-9e6e6cfa402aae1e2be9ed59d63fa3d67420841a121cef36591b5cb1e711a830R160-R245)
* Introduced a new utility function `isBlankLine` in `core/ast` to
accurately detect blank lines, with full unit test coverage.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-09beac21bb9fe8155ef13cfb9f9eaad36cdc79033a6aa71395da399b9aea7593R1-R32)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-1145765e76b7b3c5d493416c58c9b2b3dc83f27e80f7a809945e916fd65f3e63R1-R74)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-90f2ba90e1353e878c30b768c8a130270a7f57d797947f2131fe1ffb395df1dcR2-R5)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-9e6e6cfa402aae1e2be9ed59d63fa3d67420841a121cef36591b5cb1e711a830R20)
* Expanded the test suite for `consistent-code-style` to cover various
`blankLineAbove` and `blankLineBelow` scenarios, including edge cases
for start/end of file and multi-line spacing.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-d180be59aee3d4462e314f6f0469b3e9712edf410a77fa97c0bb7652376564c1R27-R28)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-d180be59aee3d4462e314f6f0469b3e9712edf410a77fa97c0bb7652376564c1R82-R316)

**Documentation and guidance improvements:**

* Added links to `markdownlint` and `remark-lint` rules in
`CONTRIBUTING.md` for easier reference.
* Updated `.github/copilot-instructions.md` with clearer monorepo
conventions, stricter PR title/description requirements, and a new
section on preferred testing style.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-227c2c26cb2ee0ce0f46a320fc48fbcbdf21801a57f59161b1d0861e8aad55f5R9-R18)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-227c2c26cb2ee0ce0f46a320fc48fbcbdf21801a57f59161b1d0861e8aad55f5L29-R87)
* Removed the now-redundant pull request prompt markdown file.

**Workflow and CI:**

* Improved the `test-cross-platform` workflow to skip draft pull
requests, ensuring only ready PRs are tested.

These changes collectively improve code style enforcement, documentation
clarity, and repository workflow consistency.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: update Top-K default from 15 to 17 for improved researcher name extraction

80-paper comparison experiment showed K=17 achieves 90% quality parity
with Standard mode (up from 70% at K=15), with the key improvement
being the ability to identify specific researcher names in cross-domain
queries. Cost remains ~1/5 of Standard mode.

Updated files:
- patch_noun_graph.py: DEFAULT_MAX_K 15 → 17
- install.sh: patch command K=15 → K=17
- README.md: updated recommendation table and examples

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  - Add AssignmentRegex (was completely missing)
  - Add GitHub token patterns (ghp_, gho_, ghs_, ghr_)
  - Add missing headers (x-finnhub-token, ocp-apim-subscription-key, x-functions-key)
  - Add missing JSON patterns (client_id, subscription_id, tenant_id, storageconnectionstring, etc.)

feat(trading-sync): Polly per-attempt timeout, signal slippage guard, reconciliation APIs

- CapitalComService: Add 15s per-attempt timeout to Polly pipeline between
  retry and circuit breaker. Prevents single hung connection from consuming
  entire 60s budget. Add TimeoutRejectedException to retry ShouldHandle.
- PaperTradingGrain: Add OpenTradeWithTimestampAsync with stale signal
  rejection (>5s) and signal-to-fill slippage measurement logging.
- TradingStrategyService: Add IsSignalFresh() staleness check helper
  with configurable MaxSignalAge for callers to validate before execution.
- TradeMemoryRepository: Add RemoveSamplesByTimeRangeAsync for order
  rejection rollback and GetSamplesInTimeRangeAsync for reconciliation.
- IPaperTradingGrain/ITradeMemoryRepository: Interface additions.

  in release builds for better interior coverage

  to catch unsorted pastSlice data that could leak future info

Initial backend setup

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Phase 8.4: Document accessibility limitations

- Created comprehensive ACCESSIBILITY.md documentation
- Analyzed AT-SPI2 requirements and D-Bus integration complexity
- Documented technical constraints for static binary architecture
- Provided workarounds: high-contrast themes, keyboard navigation
- Outlined future implementation path (6-week effort estimate)
- Decision: Document limitation per task's 'if feasible' clause

Per ROADMAP.md Phase 8.4, task allows for documenting limitations
if full implementation is not feasible. AT-SPI2 would require 2-3
weeks MVP implementation and introduces runtime dependencies
(D-Bus daemon) that conflict with static binary design goals.

Documentation provides:
- Background on AT-SPI2 protocol
- Technical constraint analysis
- Current workaround options
- Complete future implementation roadmap

This enables future contributors to implement accessibility
support if prioritized, while maintaining project's current
focus on static binary architecture.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: show locked collateral instantly on startup

Two changes for faster startup:
- Use send_utxos_updated() when loading cached UTXOs from DB at startup,
  so collateral_amount is backfilled and persisted on the very first load
- Run heavy poll (balance/txs/UTXOs) on tick 1, not just every 3rd tick,
  so fresh data from masternodes arrives within 5s instead of 15s

After the first successful UTXO sync, collateral_amount is persisted to
the masternode entries in sled. On subsequent startups, locked_balance()
reads it directly from the entries without needing a network fetch.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: time-wallet
repository.description: A next-generation cryptocurrency with 24-hour time blocks and masternode consensus

commit.message:
Fix broken calendar+bell: revert to overlay, use pointer-events toggle

Root cause of breakage: cal.click() inside click handler bubbled back
to the button, causing infinite recursion that crashed the JS event
loop — making BOTH calendar and bell completely unresponsive.

Fix:
- Restore working overlay input (position:absolute;inset:0;100x100%)
  Native tap on the invisible input opens the date picker as before
- jumpToDate(): set calendarInput.pointerEvents='none' so subsequent
  taps pass through to the button click handler (to clear the filter)
- clearDateFilter(): restore calendarInput.pointerEvents='' so the
  overlay works again for opening the picker
- Button click handler: only handles clear (fires when pointer-events
  none); opening the picker is handled by native tap on overlay
- No JS programmatic picker calls = no recursion risk

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(hardening): round 5 — 10 weaknesses fixed with TDD

W1: Dashboard.createEvalFileHandler path traversal — canonicalize + containment check
W2: SessionManager build — replace blocking stderrTask.Wait(5000) with Task.WhenAll
W3: DaemonMode.buildReplayState — activeSessionId now from live Elm model, not snapshot
W4: ManifestPersistence — invalidOp when header padLen < 0 (overflow guard)
W5: SessionManager — ProcessStartInfo.ArgumentList.Add() replaces sprintf injection
W6: McpServer — Interlocked.CompareExchange replaces non-atomic mutable logged bool
W7: SessionManager — log transport exception type/message instead of silent swallow
W8: Replay + FeatureHooks — prepend record :: history (O(1) vs O(n)); List.rev at read sites
W9: ElmLoop — ManualResetEventSlim disposed in drain thread finally; dispatch guards Set()
W10: DaemonMode.appendEventsAsync — ContinueWith logs IsFaulted tasks

Tests: Round5HardeningTests.fs — W1 path containment, W4 header overflow, W8 history order

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: correct stale prayer times on DST transition days

set_utc_time_for_date used os.time(midnight) + utc_offset to derive
midnight UTC, but on DST transition days the offset at midnight differs
from the current offset, shifting all times by 1 hour.

Rewrite to derive midnight UTC directly from the epoch via os.date(echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_OUTPUT_MARKER___ ; PS1= ; PS2= ; unset HISTFILE ; EC=0 ; echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_DONE_MARKER___0 ; }t),
independent of utc_offset. Also add @prayer-times-previous-tz-offset
cache key to invalidate stale formatted strings when the TZ offset
changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(ui/decorations): implement client-side window decorations for Phase 8.3

Add comprehensive window decorations with title bar, window controls, and resize handles:

- Created ResizeHandles widget with 8 edge/corner zones and full hit testing
- Created WindowFrame combining TitleBar and ResizeHandles components
- Implemented ResizeEdge enum with 9 states (none, 4 edges, 4 corners)
- Added hover state visual feedback for resize zone interaction
- Integrated XDG decoration protocol (zxdg_decoration_manager_v1) support
- Implemented server-side decoration negotiation (SetMode/UnsetMode)
- Added client-side fallback for decorations when server unavailable
- Updated decorations-demo to showcase full window frame capability

Test Coverage:
- 26 new tests covering resize zones, window frame, and decoration protocol
- 100% pass rate with zero critical regressions
- 50 complexity improvements in decoration subsystem

Updates ROADMAP.md Phase 8.3 completion status.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add CORS_ORIGINS to Ansible env template and setup-server workflow (#25)

## Problem

The `CORS_ORIGINS` secret was defined in `.env.example` but never wired
into the Ansible provisioning pipeline, so the server `.env` file was
always missing this variable.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `ansible/playbooks/templates/env.j2` | Add `CORS_ORIGINS={{
cors_origins }}` to the Backend section |
| `.github/workflows/setup-server.yml` | Pass `-e "cors_origins=${{
secrets.CORS_ORIGINS }}"` in both testing and production Ansible runs |

## Action required
Add `CORS_ORIGINS` to the GitHub **testing** and **production**
environment secrets:
- **testing**: `https://alumap.tst.escalopa.com`
- **production**: `https://alumap-prod.escalopa.com`

Then re-run the `setup-server` workflow to regenerate the `.env` on each
server.

Companion backend PR: iu-alumni/iu-alumni-backend#60

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
